body: PASS 10 done. Processed backlog: the 2026-07-10 SLS(34) stall directive is superseded (fixed via WARRANT#16's O(n) restructure several builds ago, the current failure mode is a totally different one -- terminal death, not stall). The 4 CARTOGRAPHER/FORGE-4D/THESIS messages were already shipped last session as WARRANT#22/23 (SLS38) -- confirmed, not re-done. The new 06:04 'TWO PROBLEMS' directive: shipped Part A in full as WARRANT#24 -> SLS(39)/9.53, compiled clean (0 errors, 1 pre-existing warning). (A1) memory telemetry at all 6 real phase-start points and every P3B regime start/end via TerminalInfoInteger(TERMINAL_MEMORY_AVAILABLE)/MQLInfoInteger(MQL_MEMORY_USED) -- found CP()'s pct==0 gate never actually fires past Phase 1, so hooked the real TLog_Head() phase markers instead. (A2) audited every array in the P3B regime loop: all static/fixed-size or freed-on-return, no regime-index-scaled growth found -- no smoking gun in static analysis, telemetry will have to catch it live. (A3) added a P3B per-regime checkpoint (TW917_<sym>_P<period>_P3BCKPT.dat) -- saved after every regime completes, loaded on start to resume from the next unfinished regime instead of losing the whole 9-regime search on a crash. (A4) no code change needed -- zero ObjectCreate/ChartRedraw exist anywhere in the file already. Part B (soft-membership refit, expectancy objective, pre-fit gating, search shrink) deliberately deferred -- it needs a full calibration to survive before its own predictions mean anything, so A has to prove out first. forge_ledger.json pass_10 filed. Per the mt4_ea_swap_automation lesson, recommend attaching SLS(39) manually via GUI rather than the automated chart-file swap for the next full run -- that's the only method that's ever produced a stable multi-hour calibration here.
